title: OpenArm work-package manifest
description: One manifest per work package, machine-read by the workflow that executes it. Field definitions are owned by docs/plan/06-추적성-레지스트리.md §2.2 and the WP-BOOT-02 contract cell of docs/plan/02a §−2.3; where this file disagrees with them, they are canonical and this file is the defect. contract_refs[] is deliberately absent: it is a derived view of consumes[] ∪ produces[] computed by the index generator, not a stored field, and unevaluatedProperties rejects it here.

exec_class.description: Closed value space (02a §0.2). Derived from workflow (00 §4.0, CI-14); only SHAPE-HG admits two values. A cell holding two tokens ('AI-offline -> AI-on-HW') is not a member of this enum and is rejected here — multi-stage belongs in phases[].

workflow.description: Closed value space (02a §0.3, vocabulary owned by 05 §1.1). Exactly one token per cell: 'SHAPE-IM + SHAPE-HG' and 'SHAPE-IM -> SHAPE-MS' are not members and are rejected — multi-stage belongs in phases[] (00 §3.2a). A sixth shape code is an error, not a translation target.

cancel_policy.description: latch-to-hold is mandatory for any stage holding rig resources: the arm has no holding brake, so 'finish the current step' means the arm keeps moving (02a §−2.3 WP-BOOT-04).

owns.description: For a multi-stage package this is the union of phases[].owns; the per-stage lists are canonical (06 §2.2).

gate_id.description: PG-RT-001 carries no meaning without its a/b suffix and is not a gate id in a value position (06 §2.3b, CI-11b); M-8 is sealed in gate-declaring positions (CI-10). Neither matches these patterns, so both are rejected wherever a gate id is a value. The seal binds field values, never prose.

contract_id.description: Single namespace CTR-<NAME>@v<n>. Semver digits are rejected: two schemas may never share one frozen generation token (CR-2, 06 §4.3).

declared_hash.description: Declared here, filled downstream by WP-N1-04 (normalization) and WP-ENV-04 (env). null means not yet issued; omitting the key entirely is rejected, because the slot has to exist before the wave that fills it can be enforced (02a §−2.3 acceptance ⑦).

phases.description: Multi-stage packages only — execution class, shape, resource claim and cancel policy change mid-package (00 §3.2a). Two stages is the minimum: a one-element phases[] describes a package whose execution meaning never changes, which is a single-stage package written the wrong way.

after.description: Predecessor stage: an index within this package, or another WP id, or null for the first stage.
